highlights.text: 6778-82, 6738-42 are key retracement pivots with a trap of 6700 being the lowest bulls want to see in a strong bull case.
These siblings are what I call “elevator down” sells.
Then ES flushes a big nearby low, recovers (Failed Breakdowns), and we rip the other way generating the second sibling, what I call the Short Squeeze.
Short Squeezes are generated by Failed Breakdowns.

I warned last Thursday at 4pm: “Watch for the Failed Breakdown of Tuesday’s 6718 daily low.” On Friday morning ES sold off 120 points,flushed last Tuesday’s 6718 low by a few points, recovered, and ripped.
We went elevator down off the futures open Sunday evening due to more bearish war headlines, selling down to 6588 from 6700+.
As always though, every elevator down sell in ES resolves in a squeeze, and Sunday evening was no different.
I wrote at the close yesterday: “We had a massive rip today driven by a big Failed Breakdown at midnight last night of the 6612 Sunday opening low.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (these are key to know), and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow.
6832, 6850 then 6918-21 are next targets.” We held/trapped 6778-82 overnight/this morning numerous times, and ripped to 6832 once overnight and again by 11am today.

post_title: Is the Bottom In For SPX? March 11 Plan
